title: Inhibition of FGFR Signaling Partially Rescues Osteoarthritis in Mice Overexpressing High Molecular Weight FGF2 Isoforms
research_question: Does in vivo treatment with the FGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor NVP-BGJ398 modulate the development of osteoarthritis (OA) phenotype in HMWTg mice overexpressing high molecular weight FGF2 isoforms?
methods_summary: Transgenic mouse model (HMWTg) overexpressing HMWFGF2 isoforms in osteoblast lineage
Daily subcutaneous injection of FGFR inhibitor NVP-BGJ398 (2 mg/kg) or vehicle for 13 weeks starting at 21 days of age
Micro-computed tomography (microCT) for subchondral bone morphometry
Histological assessment with Safranin-O staining and OARSI scoring
Alkaline phosphatase staining for calcified cartilage
Real-time quantitative RT-PCR (qRT-PCR) for gene expression
Immunohistochemistry for protein expression and localization
Serum intact FGF23 and estradiol measurement by ELISA
dataset: HMWTg transgenic mice (overexpressing HMWFGF2 isoforms) and VectorTg control mice, both male and female, FVBN strain
key_findings: BGJ treatment partially rescued irregular shape and thinning of subchondral bone in HMWTg mice, with increased BV/TV and Tb.Th in males
BGJ restored articular cartilage thickness and proteoglycan content in HMWTg mice
BGJ reduced OA degradative enzymes (MMP13, ADAMTS5) and hypertrophic markers (Col10, SOX9) in HMWTg joints
BGJ decreased elevated pFGFR1 and FGF23 expression in articular cartilage of HMWTg mice
BGJ rescued aberrant Wnt signaling by reducing WNT7B, pLRP5, AXIN2, pGSK3β, active β-catenin, and increasing DKK1 and SOST in HMWTg cartilage
extracted_insights: Overexpression of HMWFGF2 isoforms in preosteoblasts leads to osteoarthropathy in mice, which can be partially rescued by FGFR inhibitor NVP-BGJ398 through reduction of activated Wnt signaling, suggesting FGFR blockade as a potential therapeutic strategy for OA in XLH and related conditions.
